summary: President D. Todd Christofferson presented releases, new callings, and sustaining votes for General Authorities, Area Seventies, and General Officers of the Church, inviting members to express support and gratitude.
haiku:
Hands rise in covenant
Servants called, thanked, and sustained
One Church moves forward
key_points: Sustaining leaders is a sacred opportunity for Church members to show faith, unity, and support.
Releases from Church callings are occasions to express gratitude for consecrated service by leaders and their families.
The work of the Church continues through orderly succession, inspired callings, and worldwide service.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Doctrine and Covenants 26:2 — all things shall be done by common consent in the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 107:22 — the First Presidency is upheld by the confidence, faith, and prayer of the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 124:144 — officers are to be approved by the voice of the Church
Articles of Faith 1:5 — individuals are called of God by prophecy and by the laying on of hands by those in authority
Order and succession in Church leadership
questions_to_ponder: What does it mean for me personally to sustain Church leaders beyond raising my hand?
How can I better pray for and support those called to serve in the Church?
What can I learn from the faithful service of leaders who are released after many years?
How does the principle of common consent strengthen unity in the Church?
How can I respond with faith when leadership changes occur?
